source_context:
CV Assistant is an autonomous computer-vision research agent with a layered architecture.

Web Layer: A single-page web app (FastAPI, port 8420) provides the user interface. 
A CLI (cv-agent) gives terminal access. Both connect via WebSocket to the agent core.

Agent Core: LangGraph ReAct orchestrator (agent.py) powered by a local Ollama LLM.
ZeroClaw tool-execution layer wraps Python @tool functions.

Tool Layer (16 tools in 6 groups):
- Research tools: search_arxiv, fetch_paper, extract_equations, generate_spec
- Vision tools: analyze_image, describe_image (Ollama VLM + MLX Apple Silicon)
- Knowledge tools: add_to_graph, query_graph (Obsidian vault)
- Utility tools: shell, file_read, file_write, web_search
- Model tools: pull_vision_model, probe_hardware (llmfit hardware detection)
- OCR tools: run_ocr (PaddleOCR, 80+ languages)

Backend Layer:
- Ollama: local LLM/VLM inference (qwen2.5-coder, qwen3-vl)
- MLX: Apple Silicon neural-engine acceleration for SAM3 segmentation
- HuggingFace Hub: model weight downloads (SAM 2/3, SD-Turbo, SDXL, DeepGen)
- ArXiv / Semantic Scholar: paper discovery and metadata
- Local filesystem + Obsidian vault: knowledge persistence

Skills (20): Vision (SAM3 segmentation, OCR, Object Detection, Image Generation),
Content (Blog Writer, Weekly Digest), Research (Paper→Spec, Knowledge Graph),
ML/Training (Model Fine-Tuning, Dataset Analysis).
communicative_intent: End-to-end architecture of CV Assistant: Web/CLI → LangGraph ReAct agent → ZeroClaw tool layer → local AI backends (Ollama, MLX, HuggingFace).
